title: Relishing Raw Egyptian Almonds Hot-Pot
description: This sour, snappy, and energizing Egyptian dish features almonds and fermented kimchi, making it a great option for Buddhist temple-friendly meals.

ingredients.name: Fermented Kimchi
ingredients.notes: Choose a kimchi with a high umami flavor to balance the bitterness of the almonds.

ingredients.name: Fresh Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Chopped for added freshness and texture.

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.notes: Optional, add to balance the sourness of the kimchi.

instructions: Combine the raw Egyptian almonds, fermented kimchi, chopped cilantro, thinly sliced ginger, and a squeeze of lime juice in a hot pot vessel.
Mix well to ensure the ingredients are evenly distributed.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed, adding honey, salt, or black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
This dish is best served hot, straight from the pot.
If desired, add a splash of lime juice just before serving for added freshness.
For an extra kick, add a few dashes of hot sauce to the mixture before serving.
Remember to serve in a Buddhist temple-friendly vessel to show respect for the tradition.
